results.title: Huntington Bancshares AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Huntington Bancshares has emerged as a regional banking leader in AI deployment, with CFO Zachary Wasserman spearheading the bank's AI strategy[1]. The bank has deployed generative AI across 28 use cases and aims to reach 100, targeting 10-15% cost reductions and revenue increases[1]. Huntington's AI initiatives span software development, customer service, fraud detection, and lending automation, with significant investments in partnerships like Casca for AI-powered SBA lending[2]. The bank's approach emphasizes measurable ROI and operational integration, distinguishing it from the 95% of companies that MIT found are failing to achieve returns on AI investments[1].

Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Huntington National Bank
...
Specializations are machine learning, data analysis and also very interested in related applications, and statistical modeling. I am on the fence between statistics and computer science, and consider myself somewhere between a data scientist and a software engineer. I am very interested in technologies for extracting and massaging data, and using data to paint a picture of some metric of interest. I am educated in both Statistics and Computer Science and have worked on several professional and personal projects related to web mining, machine learning and data mining in general. For me, a productive work environment is one where I am challenged daily in an intellectual sense. I love exploring new concepts, new applications and new technologies that I can integrate into my position. I crave a culture where I am part of an energetic team that values each other as team members and a culture that encourages creativity, leadership, teamwork, thinking outside of the box and is reasonably flexible with its team members' needs. I really prefer environments that are open, and that allow casual attire. I enjoy building systems that solve problems possibly using methods from the latest research. Possible roles I would enjoy are, but not limited to: data scientist (with an emphasis on modeling and machine learning rather than analytics), machine learning engineer, Software engineer.

Analyze large customer and campaign datasets to identify behavioral trends, preferences, and business opportunities. Build and refine predictive models to forecast customer actions and improve acquisition and retention strategies. Design econometric and statistical models to measure the impact of campaigns and business initiatives on financial and operational metrics. Integrate diverse data sources including customer, campaign, media, and financial datasets to improve model accuracy and business relevance. Perform data preprocessing, feature engineering, hypothesis testing, and model validation to support advanced analytics solutions. Develop dashboards and business reports to track KPIs, campaign performance, and model-driven insights. Partner with marketing, analytics, and technology teams to gather requirements and deliver scalable analytical solutions. Translate complex analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for technical and non-technical stakeholders. Monitor model performance in production and continuously improve methodologies, feature design, and analytical workflows. Maintain documentation for models, methods, and workflows to support transparency, knowledge sharing, and long-term maintainability.

Automated data preprocessing, training, and deployment workflows using AWS SageMaker, AWS Lambda, and Kubeflow. Built CI/CD pipelines for ML deployments using Kubernetes and Argo CD to support reliable and scalable delivery. Developed cloud-native AI/ML solutions with a strong focus on security, scalability, and monitoring using AWS services. Used PySpark and Spark-based pipelines for large-scale data transformation, processing, and analytics. Built dashboards and visualizations using Tableau and Matplotlib to communicate insights and operational trends. Integrated Generative AI and LLM technologies into business applications for automation, personalization, and better user interaction. Contributed to RAG-based pipelines and LLM-enabled applications to improve contextual search and response generation. Worked with PostgreSQL, SQLAlchemy, AWS RDS, and cloud-based storage systems for data integration and analytics workflows. Collaborated with business stakeholders and technical teams to identify opportunities and deliver data science solutions aligned with business goals.

results.title: Pavan Kristipati
highlights:
Head of AI Engineering & Transformation (SVP Scope) Enterprise AI Adoption, Governance & Platform at [Huntington National Bank](https://www.linkedin.com/company/huntington-national-bank)
...
### Head of AI Engineering & Transformation (SVP Scope) Enterprise AI Adoption, Governance & Platform at [Huntington National Bank](https://www.linkedin.com/company/huntington-national-bank) (Current)
...
I lead AI transformation at a $285B financial institution — converting AI strategy into production-grade business outcomes in one of the most regulated environments in the world. My organization of 50+ engineers bridges the gap between AI ambition and AI reality. We don't run pilots. We industrialize AI — moving it from research into hardened, audit-ready, money-making production systems under SR 11-7, SOX, and the scrutiny of internal and external auditors. I manage a $9M+ annual operating budget through 10 direct reports across a global delivery model. ───────────────────── ✅ Enterprise AI Transformation: Built the AI Engineering function from 2 to 50+ staff in 24 months — securing C-suite capital by proving the ROI of AI industrialization. ✅ Business Impact ($80M+): Industrialized 120+ ML models across Fraud, Credit-Risk, and Marketing — translating research into measurable bottom-line revenue. ✅ Transformation Velocity: Reduced deployment cycles 6x — from 9 months to 6 weeks — by engineering a "TrustOps" pipeline satisfying every regulatory gate. ✅ AI Governance: Authored a 12-point GenAI risk framework — bias redaction, runtime monitoring, guardrail performance measurement — allowing a $285B institution to say yes to LLM innovation safely. ✅ Capital Efficiency: Delivered 30% Opex savings and $7M in vendor savings by modernizing into a cloud-portable Kubernetes platform and automating 10,000+ toil hours annually. ✅ Data Foundation: Engineered cross-cloud pipelines powering 500+ reusable features — the foundational data layer for every personalization and credit-decision model bank-wide. ───────────────────── SR 11-7
...
Appointed to the inaugural advisory board to define the security and governance standards for enterprise-grade LLM applications. My focus is on closing the "Production Gap"—ensuring that AI innovation doesn't outpace the structural integrity required by highly regulated global institutions. Architectural Governance: Moving beyond policy-based frameworks to System-Led Security. Advocating for governance that is hard-coded into the MLOps pipeline to manage the stochastic and non-deterministic risks of LLMs in real-time. The "System vs. Model" Mandate: Driving the board’s focus toward the 95% of the AI solution that isn't the model—data provenance, intent tracking, and guardrail latency—to prevent systemic failures at scale. Advising on Agentic Risk: Developing protocols for Intent Provenance in recursive agentic workflows, ensuring human accountability remains intact as autonomous systems begin to trigger and manage other AI agents. Bridging Accessibility & Security: Addressing the "Shadow AI" threat created by the collapse of the technical barrier to entry (Vibe Coding), and building frameworks that are as fast and accessible as the development tools they govern.
...
prepare the next generation
...
-level guidance

My home server setup has been expanding lately, and with it, my obsession with tinkering with local language models. My modest hardware isn't quite ready to dethrone GPT-5.5 or Opus-4.7, but the massive leaps in performance we're seeing from these smaller, local models is honestly mind-blowing.
...
If you aren't at least somewhat up-to-date on modern GenAI architecture, it's easy to miss how fast this space is moving. I spent a few weeks putting my thoughts together on quantization, hardware efficiency, and where this is all heading. The quantization part is especially interesting, given the underlying themes of Operations Research that thread through this space. 

Side note: If you have an M-series Mac, I highly recommend downloading a tool like LM Studio or Ollama and giving local models a try. You might be shocked by what your laptop can do right out of the box.

results.title: Alex Fennell
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist at [Huntington National Bank](https://www.linkedin.com/company/huntington-national-bank)
...
PhD graduate in Cognitive Psychology from The Ohio State University with over a decade of data wrangling experience. My research has revolved around applying a stochastic model of decision making, the diffusion decision model, to understand how visual working memory is represented in mind and how this representation influences the decision making process. Investigating such a fundamental aspect of human cognition is key to understanding how people interact with the world. I have a passion for learning and improving myself especially when it comes to understanding data. I am always trying to learn more about about statistical, computational, and machine learning methods in order to examine a dataset from as many perspectives as possible. Through coursework I have learned many statistical techniques such as multivariate linear regression, logistic regression, and Bayesian analysis and what questions these techniques can answer. I have applied these methodologies to class projects in addition to some of my publications. I have taken it upon myself to learn the best ways in which to visualize results in order to help convey a story I'm trying to tell. This is the most exciting part of the data lifecycle, making sense of results and creating a cohesive and engaging story. I have much experience with this, whether it is presenting at conferences, preparing a manuscript for publication, discussing a class project or lecturing a class. In relation to implementing these analyses and visualizations, R is my programming language of choice, but I have learned many other languages such as MATLAB, Python, and bash scripting when R fell short. I understand different languages have different strengths and I have to be flexible in order to best serve my purpose. I am looking to enter industry in the Data Science (or related) field. If you would like to chat or discuss job opportunities feel free to email me at afenne4@gmail.com.

### B-SOiD, an open-source unsupervised algorithm for identification and fast prediction of behaviors
...
Studying naturalistic animal behavior remains a difficult objective. Recent machine learning advances have enabled limb localization; however, extracting behaviors requires ascertaining the spatiotemporal patterns of these positions. To provide a link from poses to actions and their kinematics, we developed B-SOiD - an open-source, unsupervised algorithm that identifies behavior without user bias. By training a machine classifier on pose pattern statistics clustered using new methods, our approach achieves greatly improved processing speed and the ability to generalize across subjects or labs. Using a frameshift alignment paradigm, B-SOiD overcomes previous temporal resolution barriers. Using only a single, off-the-shelf camera, B-SOiD provides categories of sub-action for trained behaviors and kinematic measures of individual limb trajectories in any animal model. These behavioral and kinematic measures are difficult but critical to obtain, particularly in the study of rodent and other models of pain, OCD, and movement disorders. Show less

results.title: Artificial Intelligence Solutions Director | Wfh
highlights:
Artificial Intelligence Solutions Director | Wfh
...
# Artificial Intelligence Solutions Director
...
About this role: Wells Fargo is seeking an Artificial Intelligence Solutions Director, as part of the Artificial Intelligence team. Learn more about career areas and lines of business at wellsfargojobs.com. The Artificial Intelligence Solutions Director leads the enterprise-wide AI vision and strategy by managing a comprehensive AI roadmap aligned to business priorities, shaping governance and responsible AI practices, and driving market‑informed innovation. This role oversees the development and scaling of advanced AI capabilities, including machine learning, NLP, RPA, predictive analytics, and generative AI, while ensuring strict standards for security, fairness, ethics, and risk management. You will build strong cross‑functional partnerships across technology, business, operations, and risk to influence senior leaders, secure alignment, and representing the AI organization in key executive and regulatory forums. You will lead a high‑performing team of AI professionals, foster a culture of innovation and continuous learning, manage people and budget resources, and ensure successful delivery of large‑scale AI initiatives that meet regulatory, operational, and strategic business objectives. In this role, you will: Manage and develop the overall Artificial Intelligence roadmap for the company Provide leadership, management and direction companywide on artificial intelligence initiatives Develop market strategies for the company Lead Artificial Intelligence team by making strategic decisions to resolve robotics process automation, natural language processing, and machine learning Understand the options in the market and educating companywide on these opportunities Collaborate and influence all levels of professionals, including more experienced managers Lead team to achieve objectives Manage allocation of people and financial resources for Artificial Intelligence Solutions Develop and guide a culture of talent development to meet business objectives and strategy
...

results.title: Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning | Wfh
highlights:
Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning | Wfh
...
# Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
...
### About the role:
...
Angi is seeking an exceptional Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning to be a driving force behind Angi's transformation. The role is to be a leader in our Artificial Intelligence team, tackling challenges such as integrating Angi's search with LLM Providers like OpenAI GPT and Google Gemini. This is a leadership role, leading a team of dedicated machine learning engineers to both develop ML prototypes and then take them to production. In addition to being a leader, we're also looking for someone who can dive into tech and get their hands dirty to solve some of Angi's biggest technical challenges.
...
- Leadership: Lead and grow a team of talented machine learning engineers, providing guidance, mentorship, and support to foster professional growth. For our most complex challenges, you will dive into the tech to work through solutioning alongside your team.
...
- LLM Deployment and Optimization: Oversee the fine-tuning and deployment of LLMs, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an agentic framework, with a focus on optimizing model performance and scalability to enhance user experience and drive business results.
...
- Integration with 3rd Party AI APIs: Oversee the integration and management of AI tools and third-party solutions, including OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Meta AI, and Salesforce AI, to enhance user experience and business performance.
...
- Engineering & Product Collaboration: Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to build robust, scalable real-time APIs, and work on the entire development process to seamlessly integrate AI solutions into our products.
...
- Innovation: Foster innovation within the team, exploring new approaches and techniques to solve complex business problems.
...
- Who you are
...
- You have a Master's or Ph.D. in a computer science or quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related fields).
- You have 10+ years of experience in machine learning & engineering, with a focus on large-scale distributed application architecture for deploying ML models to production.
- You are an expert in machine learning deployment, API development, and real-time ML workloads.
- You are proficient in Python and comfortable diving deep into technical challenges alongside your team.
- You have excellent communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
...
Our hiring process may utilize artificial intelligence (AI) tools to assist in candidate screening and assessment. Our AI tools are designed to complement, not replace, human decision-making.
